refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies implementation. Replacing it with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's capability without specifying how. The FR contains a single obligation focused on energy efficiency target achievement, so it is atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish predictive maintenance to ensure energy-efficient operation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational parameters may influence energy use but are managed separately
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and control, which can be modularized independently from other system functions, supporting decoupling.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and reliable energy measurement is feasible within system constraints
Feedback control algorithms can respond effectively within operational timeframes
Energy efficiency targets are achievable through control adjustments alone
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including operational parameter optimization as a separate DP if control alone cannot meet energy targets, to maintain modularity and clarity in design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Contains physical energy consumption but lacks dedicated monitoring and control logic, causing moderate diffusion.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy management; would cause high diffusion.
Safety may use energy data but is not responsible for monitoring/control; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control due to its focus on energy management, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause minor interface complexity.
Integration with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces requires careful design to avoid latency or data loss impacting real-time control.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target by enabling measurement and control mechanisms necessary to meet the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target criterion.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause minor interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. External interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) are appropriate for real-time data exchange and control, minimizing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the energy_per_ton metric over the evaluation period using data collected and controlled within the NGHE Solution module, supported by Telemetry interface data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly aligns with the FR and DP, with well-defined scope and interfaces, ensuring clear allocation of responsibility for energy efficiency achievement.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize the interface with the Vehicle module to ensure reliable and timely raw energy data acquisition, minimizing integration complexity.
Design robust communication protocols over Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to prevent latency or data loss impacting real-time feedback control.
Document contingency plans for handling data delays or failures in external interfaces to maintain control performance and safety compliance.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear alignment and containment, and verification feasibility is established. The recommended revisions pertain to interface clarifications and communication robustness, which are implementation details rather than new functional requirements. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize the current level with these revisions rather than decomposing further, to maintain focus on integration and verification.

revise.why: Clarify and formalize the interface with the Vehicle module and design robust communication protocols over Telemetry and Network Link interfaces to ensure reliable data acquisition and control, addressing integration complexity and safety concerns.

assumptions: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' fully satisfies the top-level FR.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate ownership boundary for the DP implementation.
Telemetry and Network Link interfaces are sufficient for real-time data exchange.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause minor interface complexity.
Risk of latency or data loss impacting real-time feedback control.
Handling data delays or failures in external interfaces to maintain control performance and safety compliance.
evidence_needed: Formal interface specifications with the Vehicle module.
Communication protocol designs and validation results.
Contingency plans and their verification for data delays or failures.
